example.meaning: Please, go ahead and eat.

example.meaning: Have you looked at this document?

example.meaning: I know very well who you are, sensei.

example.meaning: I have read your letter.

example.meaning: I will visit your company tomorrow.

example.meaning: We make a judgment in light of past cases.

example.meaning: He entered by deceiving his identity.

example.meaning: Deceiving people is not acceptable.

example.meaning: He speaks in a way that shows off his knowledge.
example.meaningKo: 그는 지식을 뽐내는 듯한 말투를 한다.

example.meaning: I was so preoccupied with playing that I forgot to study.

example.meaning: Please refrain from whispering during the meeting.

example.sentence: 言葉を謹んで話すべきだ。
example.meaning: You should speak with discretion.

example.sentence: 上司の前で意見を言うのを憚った。
example.meaning: I hesitated to express my opinion in front of my boss.

example.meaning: I gave up on quitting the company after being persuaded by a friend.
example.meaningKo: 친구의 설득으로 회사를 그만두는 것을 단념했다.

example.meaning: The retainer remonstrated with the king over his mistake.

example.meaning: The teacher gently rebuked the student who was late.
furigana: をやさしくたしなめた

example.sentence: 他人の努力を貶めるべきではない。
example.meaning: You should not belittle the efforts of others.

example.sentence: 相手を見くびると痛い目に遭うぞ。
example.meaning: If you underestimate your opponent, you will regret it.

example.sentence: 敵を侮ってはいけない。
example.meaning: You must not despise your enemy.

example.sentence: 人を外見で蔑むのは間違っている。
example.meaning: It is wrong to look down on people based on their appearance.
example.meaningKo: 사람을 외모로 멸시하는 것은 잘못된 것이다.

example.sentence: 彼女の才能は群衆の中で際立っている。
example.meaning: Her talent stands out in the crowd.

example.sentence: 彼の赤いジャケットはとても目立つ。
example.meaning: His red jacket is very conspicuous.
example.meaningKo: 그의 빨간 재킷은 매우 눈에 띈다.

example.sentence: 彼は数学において秀でている。
example.meaning: He excels in mathematics.

example.sentence: 経験は知識に勝ることもある。
example.meaning: Experience can sometimes surpass knowledge.

example.sentence: この製品は品質において他社に劣る。
example.meaning: This product is inferior to other companies in quality.
example.meaningKo: 이 제품은 품질 면에서 다른 회사에 뒤떨어진다.

example.sentence: 彼の実力はプロ選手に匹敵する。
example.meaning: His ability is a match for professional players.

example.sentence: その選手は世界記録を凌駕した。
example.meaning: That athlete surpassed the world record.

example.sentence: 寒い冬をなんとか凌いだ。
example.meaning: We somehow managed to endure the cold winter.

example.sentence: 今月の売上は予想を上回った。
example.meaning: This month's sales exceeded expectations.

example.sentence: 気温が平年を下回る日が続いている。
example.meaning: Days with temperatures falling below average continue.
example.meaningKo: 기온이 평년을 밑도는 날이 계속되고 있다.

5.meaning: to go back in time; to trace back
5.subject: 이동·변화 동사

example.sentence: 歴史を遡ると、様々な文化が見えてくる。
example.meaning: If you trace back through history, you can see various cultures.
example.meaningKo: 역사를 거슬러 올라가면 다양한 문화가 보인다.

example.sentence: 長い旅の末、ついに目的地に行き着いた。
example.meaning: After a long journey, I finally arrived at my destination.
example.meaningKo: 긴 여행 끝에 마침내 목적지에 도달했다.

5.meaning: to finally reach; to manage to arrive
5.subject: 이동·변화 동사

example.sentence: 迷いながらも、なんとか駅に辿り着いた。
example.meaning: Although I got lost, I somehow managed to reach the station.

example.sentence: 祖先の足跡を辿る旅に出た。
example.meaning: I set out on a journey to trace the footsteps of my ancestors.

5.meaning: to move around from place to place
5.subject: 이동·변화 동사

example.sentence: 彼は若い頃、世界中を渡り歩いた。
example.meaning: When he was young, he traveled all around the world.
example.meaningKo: 그는 젊었을 때 전 세계를 돌아다녔다.

example.sentence: 京都の名所を巡るツアーに参加した。
example.meaning: I joined a tour going around the famous spots of Kyoto.

5.meaning: to happen to meet; to encounter by chance
5.subject: 이동·변화 동사

example.sentence: 素晴らしい先生に巡り合えて、本当に幸運だった。
example.meaning: I was truly lucky to happen to meet such a wonderful teacher.
example.meaningKo: 훌륭한 선생님을 우연히 만날 수 있어서 정말 행운이었다.

example.sentence: 話題を転じて、別の問題について話し合った。
example.meaning: We shifted the topic and discussed a different issue.
example.meaningKo: 화제를 전환하여 다른 문제에 대해 이야기했다.

5.meaning: to change over time; to undergo transition
5.subject: 이동·변화 동사

example.sentence: 言葉の意味は時代とともに変遷する。
example.meaning: The meanings of words change over time.

example.sentence: 季節が移り変わるにつれて、景色も変わっていく。
example.meaning: As the seasons change, the scenery changes too.
example.meaningKo: 계절이 변천함에 따라 경치도 바뀌어 간다.

example.sentence: 彼の言葉が私をためらわせる。
example.meaning: His words make me hesitate.

example.sentence: 複雑な地図が私を迷わせる。
example.meaning: The complicated map confuses me.

example.sentence: 突然の質問が彼女を戸惑わせる。
example.meaning: The sudden question perplexes her.

example.sentence: 大きな犬を見ておじけづいた。
example.meaning: I lost my nerve when I saw the big dog.

example.sentence: 難しい課題を前に尻込みしてしまった。
example.meaning: I shrank back when faced with the difficult task.

example.sentence: 彼はためらいなく手を挙げた。
example.meaning: He raised his hand without hesitation.

example.sentence: 試合に負けたが、潔く諦めることにした。
example.meaning: I lost the match, but decided to give up gracefully.
example.meaningKo: 경기에 졌지만 미련 없이 포기하기로 했다.

example.sentence: 長い間悩んだが、ようやく踏ん切りをつけた。
example.meaning: I worried for a long time, but finally made up my mind.

example.sentence: 思い切りよく新しい仕事に挑戦した。
example.meaning: I decisively took on a new job.

example.sentence: 失敗を恐れずに大胆にやることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to do things boldly without fear of failure.
example.meaningKo: 실패를 두려워하지 않고 대담하게 하는 것이 중요하다.

8.meaning: to spread rampantly, to run rife
8.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 都市に犯罪がはびこっている。
example.meaning: Crime is running rife in the city.

example.sentence: 感染症が世界中に蔓延する恐れがある。
example.meaning: There is a fear that the infectious disease will spread throughout the world.
example.meaningKo: 감염병이 전 세계에 만연할 우려가 있다.

example.sentence: その噂はすぐに町中に広まった。
example.meaning: The rumor spread throughout the town quickly.
example.meaningKo: 그 소문은 금방 온 동네에 퍼졌다.

example.sentence: 新しい文化が社会に浸透する。

8.meaning: to become established, to take root
8.subject: 동사

example.sentence: リモートワークが新しい働き方として定着した。
example.meaning: Remote work has become established as a new way of working.

example.sentence: スマートフォンが世界中に普及した。
example.meaning: Smartphones have spread throughout the world.

example.sentence: その情報はSNSで急速に拡散した。
example.meaning: The information spread rapidly on social media.

example.sentence: 警察は暴動を抑え込んだ。
example.meaning: The police suppressed the riot.

example.sentence: 軍は反乱軍を完全に制圧した。

example.meaning: She tackled her goal earnestly.

example.sentence: 彼は毎日勉強に励んでいる。

example.sentence: 彼女は毎朝練習に勤しんでいる。

9.meaningKo: 手を抜く/手を抜く/대충 하다

example.meaningKo: 일을 대충 하면 신뢰를 잃어버리게 된다.

example.sentence: 古い建物が左に傾いている。

10.meaning: to be biased, to be one-sided
10.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 彼の意見はいつも一方に偏っている。

example.sentence: 矢が的から逸れてしまった。

example.sentence: ボタンが外れてしまった。

example.sentence: 熱でプラスチックが歪んでしまった。
example.meaning: The plastic warped due to the heat.

10.meaning: to be distorted, to be warped

example.sentence: 彼らはその土地を長年占有している。

example.meaningKo: 그들은 그 토지를 오랫동안 점유하고 있다.

example.sentence: その会社は市場を独占している。
example.meaning: That company monopolizes the market.

11.meaning: to share, to have in common
11.subject: 동사

example.sentence: チームでファイルを共有する。

11.meaning: to share, to divide among each other
11.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 喜びを皆で分かち合う。

11.meaning: to share the burden, to divide responsibilities
11.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 家事を家族で分担する。
example.meaning: We divide household chores among the family.

example.sentence: 利益をメンバーに分配する。

example.sentence: 予算を各部署に配分する。

11.meaning: to average, to calculate the mean

12.meaning: to give up on, to abandon
12.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 医者に見放された患者が奇跡的に回復した。
example.meaning: A patient who was given up on by the doctor miraculously recovered.

12.meaning: to give up on, to cut one's losses
12.subject: 동사

example.sentence: その事業を見切って撤退することにした。
example.meaning: I decided to give up on that business and withdraw.

12.meaning: to give up on, to decide to abandon
12.subject: 동사

12.meaning: to ascertain, to see through to the end
12.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 相手の真意を見極めることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to see through the other person's true intentions.

12.meaning: to watch until the end, to witness
12.subject: 동사

example.meaningKo: 딸의 결혼식을 끝까지 지켜보고 아버지는 안심했다.

example.sentence: 彼は将来を見通す力を持っている。
example.meaning: He has the ability to foresee the future.
example.meaningKo: 그는 미래를 꿰뚫어보는 능력을 가지고 있다.

example.sentence: 利益を見込んで新しいプロジェクトを始めた。
example.meaning: We started a new project expecting profit.

example.sentence: 回復の見込みがあると医者に言われた。
example.meaning: The doctor told me there is a prospect of recovery.

12.meaning: to have no prospect, to be hopeless
12.subject: 표현

example.sentence: このプロジェクトは成功の見込みがないと判断した。
example.meaning: I judged that this project has no prospect of success.
example.meaningKo: 이 프로젝트는 성공할 가망이 없다고 판단했다.

13.meaning: to chew thoroughly; to savor deeply
13.subject: 감각/심리 동사

example.sentence: 彼女の言葉を噛みしめながら、静かに考えた。

example.sentence: 母は子どもたちを深く慈しんでいた。
example.meaning: The mother tenderly cherished her children.

example.sentence: 先生は生徒たちの才能を育んだ。

13.meaning: to watch over; to keep an eye on
13.subject: 관찰 동사

example.sentence: 親は子どもの成長を温かく見守る。

13.meaning: to protect; to cover for someone
13.subject: 보호 동사

example.meaningKo: 친구의 실수를 감싸고 스스로 책임을 졌다.

13.meaning: to persistently cover for someone; to defend stubbornly
13.subject: 보호 동사

example.sentence: どんな状況でも彼を庇い立てるのはよくない。
example.meaning: It is not good to persistently defend him no matter the situation.
example.meaningKo: 어떤 상황에서도 그를 감싸고 두둔하는 것은 좋지 않다.

13.meaning: to value; to respect; to place importance on
13.subject: 태도 동사

example.sentence: 彼は伝統を重んじる人物だ。
example.meaning: He is a person who values tradition.

13.meaning: to revere; to hold in high esteem
13.subject: 태도 동사

example.sentence: 命を尊ぶ心を忘れてはいけない。

example.meaningKo: 생명을 소중히 여기는 마음을 잊어서는 안 된다.

example.sentence: 政府は脱税を厳しく取り締まる方針だ。

example.sentence: 政府は有害な化学物質の使用を規制する。
example.meaning: The government regulates the use of harmful chemical substances.

example.sentence: 法律で基準を定める。
example.meaning: Standards are established by law.

example.sentence: 会社は新しい規則を設ける。
example.meaning: The company establishes new rules.

example.sentence: 駅の構内に監視カメラを設置する。
example.meaning: Security cameras are installed inside the station.

example.sentence: 古い制度を廃止する。
example.meaning: The old system is abolished.

14.meaning: to revise, to amend (prices or rules)
14.subject: 법률/행정

example.sentence: 来月から料金を改定する予定だ。
example.meaning: The fees are scheduled to be revised from next month.

14.meaning: to revise, to amend (laws or regulations)
14.subject: 법률/행정

example.meaning: The parliament continues to debate revising the constitution.

example.meaningKo: 정부는 재생 가능 에너지의 이용을 장려한다.

example.sentence: 音楽は心の痛みを和らげる。
example.meaning: Music alleviates the pain in one's heart.

example.sentence: 政府は規制を緩和する方針を示した。
example.meaning: The government indicated a policy to ease regulations.

example.sentence: 自然の風景が疲れた心を癒す。

example.sentence: 泣いている子供を宥める。

example.sentence: 深呼吸して怒りを鎮める。
example.meaning: I take a deep breath to calm my anger.

example.sentence: 先生は騒いでいる生徒たちを落ち着かせる。

example.sentence: 冷えたスープを温める。

15.meaning: to reheat, to warm up again
15.subject: 동작 동사

example.sentence: 冷めた料理をレンジで温め直す。
example.meaning: I reheat the cooled dish in the microwave.

example.sentence: 雨が大地を潤す。
example.meaning: The rain moistens the earth.

example.sentence: オークションで値段が競り上がった。
example.meaning: The price soared at the auction.

16.meaning: to rise sharply; to go up
16.subject: 가격/변동

example.sentence: 物価が吊り上がって生活が苦しくなった。
example.meaning: Prices rose sharply and life became difficult.

example.sentence: 燃料費が跳ね上がり、企業の負担が増えた。
example.meaning: Fuel costs surged, increasing the burden on companies.

example.sentence: 原油価格が急騰して、各国の経済に影響を与えた。
example.meaning: Crude oil prices skyrocketed and affected economies around the world.
example.meaningKo: 원유 가격이 급등하여 각국 경제에 영향을 미쳤다.

example.sentence: 株価が急落して、投資家たちは慌てた。
example.meaning: Stock prices plunged and investors panicked.

example.sentence: 下落していた株価がようやく持ち返した。
example.meaning: The falling stock prices finally recovered.

16.meaning: to crawl up; to claw one's way back
16.subject: 노력/재기

example.sentence: 彼は失敗を乗り越えて、底辺から這い上がった。
example.meaning: He overcame his failures and clawed his way back from the bottom.

16.meaning: to go back to square one
16.subject: 상황/전개

example.sentence: 交渉が決裂して、振り出しに戻ってしまった。
example.meaning: The negotiations broke down and we were back to square one.

16.meaning: to make a comeback; to turn the tables
16.subject: 경쟁/역전

example.sentence: 後半戦で猛攻をかけ、相手チームを巻き返した。
example.meaning: They launched a fierce attack in the second half and turned the tables on the opposing team.
example.meaningKo: 후반전에 맹공을 퍼부어 상대 팀을 역전했다.

16.meaning: to close the gap; to catch up to
16.subject: 경쟁/추격

example.sentence: 2位の選手が猛スピードでトップを追い上げた。
example.meaning: The second-place runner chased down the leader at full speed.

example.sentence: 彼の言葉が好奇心を掻き立てる。
example.meaning: His words stir up curiosity.

example.sentence: 群衆を煽る演説は危険だ。
example.meaning: A speech that incites the crowd is dangerous.

example.sentence: 友人が悪いことをするよう唆した。
example.meaning: A friend instigated me to do something bad.

17.meaning: to set someone on, to egg on
17.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 兄は弟に喧嘩をけしかけた。
example.meaning: The older brother egged his younger brother into a fight.

example.sentence: 先生は生徒に夢を吹き込んだ。
example.meaning: The teacher instilled dreams into the students.

example.sentence: その映画は強い感情を喚起する。
example.meaning: That movie evokes strong emotions.

example.sentence: 新商品が潜在的な需要を引き出した。
example.meaning: The new product drew out latent demand.

example.sentence: 古い歌が懐かしい記憶を呼び起こす。
example.meaning: An old song evokes nostalgic memories.

example.sentence: その発言が大きな議論を掻き起こした。
example.meaning: That remark stirred up a great debate.

example.sentence: 不注意な運転が事故を引き起こす。
example.meaning: Careless driving causes accidents.

18.meaning: to be inferior to; to fall behind
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼の実力は誰にも引けを取らない。
example.meaning: His ability is inferior to no one.

18.meaning: to be second to none; not inferior
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼女の歌声はプロにも引けを取らない。
example.meaning: Her singing voice is not inferior even to professionals.

18.meaning: to make a difference; to create a gap
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 練習を重ねて、ライバルに差をつけた。
example.meaning: By accumulating practice, I created a gap over my rival.

18.meaning: to have a gap; a difference arises
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 努力次第で成績に差がつく。
example.meaning: Depending on effort, a difference arises in grades.
example.meaningKo: 노력 여하에 따라 성적에 격차가 난다.

18.meaning: to stand out; to excel above others
18.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 彼は数学の才能で他の生徒から抜きんでていた。
example.meaning: He stood out from the other students in his mathematical talent.
example.meaningKo: 그는 수학 재능으로 다른 학생들 사이에서 두드러졌다.

18.meaning: cannot compete with; no match for
18.meaningKo: 立ち打ちできない/당해낼 수 없다
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: あの選手には太刀打ちできない。
example.meaning: There is no way to compete with that player.

18.meaningKo: 叶わない/당해낼 수 없다/이룰 수 없다
18.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 彼の体力にはとてもかなわない。
example.meaning: I simply cannot match his physical strength.
example.meaningKo: 그의 체력에는 도저히 당해낼 수 없다.

18.meaning: to be at a complete loss; utterly helpless
18.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 問題が難しすぎて手も足も出なかった。
example.meaning: The problem was too difficult and I was completely helpless.

18.meaning: to be at a loss what to do with; to find hard to handle
18.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 子供のエネルギーを持て余している。
example.meaning: I am at a loss with the children's energy.

18.meaning: to have trouble with; to struggle with
18.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 新しいソフトウェアの操作に手こずった。
example.meaning: I had trouble with the operation of the new software.

example.sentence: 災害に備えて食料を準備した。
example.meaning: I prepared food in case of a disaster.

19.meaning: to be equipped with, to be endowed with
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 彼には生まれつき才能が備わっている。
example.meaning: He is naturally endowed with talent.
example.meaningKo: 그에게는 태어날 때부터 재능이 갖춰져 있다.

example.sentence: 毎日練習して新しい技術を身に付けた。
example.meaning: I practiced every day and acquired a new skill.

19.meaning: to become part of oneself, to be acquired
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 繰り返し練習することで知識が身に付く。
example.meaning: Knowledge becomes ingrained through repeated practice.

19.meaning: to feel keenly, to feel deeply
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 失敗して初めて親の言葉が身に染みた。
example.meaning: Only after failing did I keenly feel the weight of my parents' words.
example.meaningKo: 실패하고 나서야 비로소 부모님의 말씀이 뼈저리게 느껴졌다.

19.meaning: to learn one's lesson, to be disgusted with
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 一度失敗して懲りたので、もうやらない。
example.meaning: I learned my lesson from failing once, so I won't do it again.
example.meaningKo: 한번 실패해서 혼이 났기 때문에 이제 하지 않는다.

19.meaning: to be fed up with, to have had enough
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: あの仕事にはもうこりごりした。
example.meaning: I've had more than enough of that job.
example.meaningKo: 그 일은 이제 정말 신물이 난다.

19.meaning: to be thoroughly fed up with, to be sick and tired of
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 毎日同じ仕事で飽き飽きしている。
example.meaning: I'm thoroughly fed up with doing the same work every day.

19.meaning: to be disgusted, to be fed up
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 長い会議にうんざりした。
example.meaning: I was fed up with the long meeting.

19.meaning: to come to dislike, to get sick of
19.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 毎日残業が続いて仕事が嫌になった。
example.meaning: With overtime every day, I've come to dislike my job.

example.sentence: 彼はどんな困難もうまく切り抜ける。
example.meaning: He manages to get through any difficulty.

example.sentence: チーム全員で危機を乗り切った。
example.meaning: The whole team got through the crisis together.
example.meaningKo: 팀 전원이 힘을 합쳐 위기를 극복했다.

20.meaning: to pioneer, to open up (a path)
20.subject: 복합 동사

example.sentence: 彼女は自分の力で新しい道を切り開いた。
example.meaning: She pioneered a new path by her own effort.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 자신의 힘으로 새로운 길을 개척했다.

example.sentence: 選手たちは強豪チームを打ち破った。
example.meaning: The players broke through the powerful team.

example.sentence: 困難を乗り越えることで人は成長する。
example.meaning: People grow by overcoming difficulties.

example.sentence: 彼はすべての批判を跳ね除けた。
example.meaning: He brushed aside all criticism.

20.meaning: to shake off, to brush away
20.subject: 복합 동사

example.sentence: 不安を振り払って前へ進んだ。
example.meaning: I shook off my anxiety and moved forward.

20.meaning: to slip through, to pass through
20.subject: 복합 동사

example.sentence: 彼はすばやく人混みを擦り抜けた。
example.meaning: He quickly slipped through the crowd.

20.meaning: to get through, to slip through
20.subject: 복합 동사

example.sentence: 多くの試練を潜り抜けてここまで来た。
example.meaning: I have come this far by getting through many trials.

example.sentence: 二つの会社は市場で凌ぎを削っている。
example.meaning: The two companies are competing fiercely in the market.
example.meaningKo: 두 회사는 시장에서 치열하게 다투고 있다.

21.meaning: to be a hindrance, to cause inconvenience
21.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 明日来られなくても差し支えありません。
example.meaning: It's no problem even if you can't come tomorrow.

21.meaning: to be no problem, to be fine
21.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 差し支えなければ、理由を教えてください。
example.meaning: If it's no trouble, please tell me the reason.

example.sentence: 騒音が勉強を妨げる。
example.meaning: Noise hinders studying.

example.sentence: 敵の前進を阻む。
example.meaning: Block the enemy's advance.

21.meaning: to be diminished, to have one's spirit dampened
21.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 批判されて意欲が削がれた。
example.meaning: My motivation was dampened by the criticism.

example.sentence: その一言がやる気を削いだ。
example.meaning: That one word dampened my motivation.

example.sentence: 彼の夢を挫くようなことは言いたくない。
example.meaning: I don't want to say anything that would crush his dreams.
example.meaningKo: 그의 꿈을 꺾는 말은 하고 싶지 않다.

21.meaning: to be discouraged, to give up
21.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 何度失敗しても挫けずに挑戦し続けた。
example.meaning: No matter how many times I failed, I continued to challenge myself without giving up.
example.meaningKo: 몇 번을 실패해도 좌절하지 않고 도전을 계속했다.

21.meaning: to not give up, to not be discouraged
21.subject: 동사

example.sentence: どんな困難にも挫けない強さを持っている。
example.meaning: He has the strength to not give up no matter what difficulties arise.
example.meaningKo: 어떤 어려움에도 좌절하지 않는 강인함을 가지고 있다.

example.sentence: もう少しだから踏ん張ろう。
example.meaning: We're almost there, so let's hang in there.

22.meaning: to interfere; to throw cold water on
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: せっかくの楽しい雰囲気に水を差すようなことを言わないでください。
example.meaning: Please don't say things that throw cold water on the enjoyable atmosphere.
example.meaningKo: 모처럼의 즐거운 분위기에 찬물을 끼얹는 말은 하지 마세요.

22.meaning: to hold someone back; to drag someone down
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: チームの足を引っ張らないように、もっと練習しなければならない。
example.meaning: I have to practice more so as not to hold the team back.
example.meaningKo: 팀의 발목을 잡지 않도록 더 연습해야 한다.

example.sentence: 二人の会話に口を挟むのは失礼だと思った。
example.meaning: I thought it was rude to butt into the two people's conversation.
example.meaningKo: 두 사람의 대화에 끼어드는 것은 실례라고 생각했다.

22.meaning: to stick one's nose into; to meddle
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 彼は何でも首を突っ込みたがる性格だ。
example.meaning: He has a personality that makes him want to stick his nose into everything.
example.meaningKo: 그는 무엇이든 참견하고 싶어 하는 성격이다.

22.meaning: to discourage someone at the outset; to nip in the bud
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 張り切って出発しようとしたら、雨に出鼻を挫かれた。
example.meaning: Just as I was eager to set off, I was discouraged right at the start by the rain.
example.meaningKo: 의욕 넘치게 출발하려 했더니 비에 기세가 꺾였다.

22.meaning: to outwit someone; to make a fool of someone
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: ライバルの鼻を明かしてやろうと、必死に準備した。
example.meaning: I prepared desperately to outwit my rival.
example.meaningKo: 라이벌의 코를 납작하게 해주려고 필사적으로 준비했다.

22.meaning: to be outwitted; to be made a fool of
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 自信満々だったのに、後輩に鼻を明かされて恥ずかしかった。
example.meaning: I was so confident, but I was outwitted by my junior and felt embarrassed.
example.meaningKo: 자신만만했는데 후배에게 코를 납작하게 당해서 부끄러웠다.

22.meaning: to leave a pause; to leave an interval
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 少し間を置いてから、彼女は静かに答えた。
example.meaning: After leaving a short pause, she answered quietly.
example.meaningKo: 잠시 간격을 두고 나서, 그녀는 조용히 대답했다.

22.meaning: to take a pause; to find a middle ground
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 話すときは適度に間を取ると、聞き手に伝わりやすくなる。
example.meaning: When speaking, taking appropriate pauses makes it easier for the listener to understand.
example.meaningKo: 말할 때 적절히 간격을 두면 듣는 사람에게 전달하기 쉬워진다.

22.meaning: to make do; to manage with what is available
22.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 材料が足りなかったので、あるものを使って間に合わせた。
example.meaning: Since the ingredients were not enough, I made do with what was available.
example.meaningKo: 재료가 부족해서 있는 것으로 임시방편으로 했다.

example.sentence: 彼の回復のために手を尽くした。
example.meaning: I did everything possible for his recovery.
example.meaningKo: 그의 회복을 위해 모든 수를 썼다.

23.meaning: to start; to begin working on
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: まだ宿題に手をつけていない。
example.meaning: I haven't started on my homework yet.

23.meaning: to cut ties; to sever a relationship
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 彼はその組織と手を切った。
example.meaning: He severed his ties with the organization.

example.sentence: 彼女はそのプロジェクトから手を引くことにした。
example.meaning: She decided to withdraw from the project.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 그 프로젝트에서 손을 떼기로 했다.

23.meaning: to receive help; to borrow someone's hand
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 引っ越しで友人の手を借りた。
example.meaning: I received help from a friend when moving.

23.meaning: to lend a hand; to help
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 困っている人に手を貸すのは当然だ。
example.meaning: It is natural to lend a hand to someone in trouble.
example.meaningKo: 어려움에 처한 사람에게 도움을 주는 것은 당연하다.

23.meaning: to have trouble dealing with; to be at a loss
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 先生はいたずらな生徒に手を焼いている。
example.meaning: The teacher is having trouble dealing with the mischievous student.
example.meaningKo: 선생님은 장난꾸러기 학생에게 애를 먹고 있다.

23.meaningKo: 手に負えない/감당이 안 되다
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: あの子は手に負えないほどわがままだ。
example.meaning: That child is so selfish that they are unmanageable.
example.meaningKo: 저 아이는 감당이 안 될 정도로 제멋대로이다.

23.meaning: to be unable to handle; to be at a loss
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 親も手を余すほど彼は問題を起こした。
example.meaning: He caused so many problems that even his parents were unable to handle him.
example.meaningKo: 부모도 다루기 힘들 정도로 그는 문제를 일으켰다.

23.meaning: to be short-handed; to lack workers
23.subject: 관용구

example.sentence: 繁忙期は手が足りなくて大変だ。
example.meaning: It is tough during the busy season because we are short-handed.

example.sentence: 彼女はいつも周りの人に気を遣う。
example.meaning: She is always considerate of the people around her.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 항상 주변 사람들에게 신경을 쓴다.

24.meaning: to pay attention to everything around
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 先生は生徒全員に気を配る。
example.meaning: The teacher pays attention to all the students.

24.meaning: to brace oneself; to keep one's spirits up
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: ずっと気を張っていたので、疲れてしまった。
example.meaning: I was so tense the whole time that I got exhausted.

24.meaning: to pull oneself together; to regain composure
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 失敗しても気を取り直して、もう一度挑戦した。
example.meaning: Even after failing, I pulled myself together and tried again.
example.meaningKo: 실패해도 기분을 다잡고 다시 한 번 도전했다.

24.meaning: to recover one's spirits; to cheer up
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 友達に励まされて、気を持ち直した。
example.meaning: Encouraged by my friend, I recovered my spirits.

24.meaning: to be reluctant; to not feel like doing something
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: その会議には気が進まないが、参加しなければならない。
example.meaning: I'm reluctant to attend that meeting, but I have to go.
example.meaningKo: 그 회의에는 내키지 않지만 참가해야 한다.

24.meaning: to not like; to be dissatisfied with
24.meaningKo: 気に入らない/마음에 들지 않다
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼の態度がどうしても気に入らない。
example.meaning: I just cannot stand his attitude.
example.meaningKo: 그의 태도가 아무리 봐도 마음에 들지 않는다.

24.meaning: to weigh on one's mind; to be worried about
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 昨日言ってしまったことが気にかかる。
example.meaning: What I said yesterday weighs on my mind.

24.meaning: to offend; to irritate; to get on one's nerves
24.subject: 気 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼の一言が気に障った。
example.meaning: His one remark got on my nerves.

example.sentence: 試験の結果が出るまで気を揉んだ。
example.meaning: I fretted until the exam results came out.
example.meaningKo: 시험 결과가 나올 때까지 마음을 졸였다.

25.meaning: to exhaust oneself / to give one's all
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は身を削って家族のために働いた。
example.meaning: He worked with everything he had for his family.
example.meaningKo: 그는 혼신을 다해 가족을 위해 일했다.

25.meaning: to make a painful sacrifice / to cut to the bone
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼女は身を切る思いで会社を辞めた。
example.meaning: She resigned from the company with a painful heart.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 뼈를 깎는 심정으로 회사를 그만뒀다.

25.meaning: to dedicate oneself / to throw oneself into
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は研究に身を投げうって取り組んだ。
example.meaning: He threw himself into his research.

25.meaning: to sacrifice oneself / to offer one's body
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 消防士は身を呈して人々を救った。
example.meaning: The firefighter sacrificed himself to save people.

25.meaning: to give one's all / to devote oneself entirely
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は国のために身を尽くした。
example.meaning: He gave his all for his country.
example.meaningKo: 그는 나라를 위해 온 힘을 다했다.

25.meaning: to act as a substitute / to take someone's place
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: 彼は友人の身代わりになって罰を受けた。
example.meaning: He took the punishment in place of his friend.
example.meaningKo: 그는 친구의 대신이 되어 벌을 받았다.

25.meaning: to be more than one deserves / to be overwhelming
25.subject: 관용 표현

example.sentence: このような賞をいただくのは身に余る光栄です。
example.meaning: Receiving such an award is an honor beyond what I deserve.

example.meaningKo: 나중에 문제가 될 테니, 지금 미리 확인해 두자.

example.sentence: さきざきのことを考えて、貯金を始めた。
example.meaning: Thinking about the future, I started saving money.

example.sentence: これはまさしく私が探していたものだ。
example.meaning: This is exactly what I was looking for.

example.sentence: この成功はひとえに皆さんのおかげです。
example.meaning: This success is solely thanks to all of you.

example.sentence: 彼はもっぱら読書に時間を費やしている。
example.meaning: He spends his time exclusively on reading.
example.meaningKo: 그는 오로지 독서에 시간을 쏟고 있다.

example.sentence: 彼女の成長は著しい。
example.meaning: Her growth is remarkable.

example.sentence: 彼の活躍は目覚ましい。
example.meaning: His performance is stunning.

example.sentence: 今回の変化は著しくはない。
example.meaning: The change this time is not remarkable.

example.sentence: 僅かな差で負けた。
example.meaning: I lost by a slight margin.

example.sentence: 二人の関係は微妙な雰囲気だった。
example.meaning: The atmosphere between the two was subtle.

example.sentence: 幽かな光が見えた。
example.meaning: I could see a faint light.

example.sentence: 二つの意見には画然とした違いがある。
example.meaning: There is a clear-cut difference between the two opinions.
example.meaningKo: 두 의견 사이에는 확연한 차이가 있다.

example.sentence: それは明らかな間違いだ。
example.meaning: That is an obvious mistake.

example.sentence: 彼の返事は曖昧な表現だった。
example.meaning: His reply was a vague expression.

example.sentence: 彼女の笑顔から本音が垣間見える。
example.meaning: Her true feelings can be glimpsed through her smile.

example.sentence: その事件はあまりにもむごい結末を迎えた。
example.meaning: That incident met an all too cruel end.
example.meaningKo: 그 사건은 너무나도 끔찍한 결말을 맞이했다.

example.sentence: 残酷な運命が彼女を襲った。
example.meaning: A cruel fate struck her.

example.sentence: 酷薄な彼は困っている人を見ても助けようとしない。
example.meaning: Cold-hearted as he is, he makes no attempt to help people in trouble even when he sees them.
example.meaningKo: 냉혹한 그는 곤경에 처한 사람을 봐도 도우려 하지 않는다.

example.sentence: 冷酷な上司は部下の失敗を絶対に許さない。
example.meaning: The ruthless boss never forgives his subordinates' mistakes.
example.meaningKo: 냉혹한 상사는 부하의 실수를 절대 용서하지 않는다.

example.sentence: 無慈悲な独裁者が国民を苦しめた。
example.meaning: A merciless dictator tormented the people.

example.sentence: 痛ましい事故の知らせが届いた。
example.meaning: News of a heartrending accident arrived.

example.sentence: 惨めな生活から抜け出したいと思っている。
example.meaning: I want to escape from this miserable life.
example.meaningKo: 비참한 생활에서 벗어나고 싶다고 생각하고 있다.

example.sentence: 哀れな子犬が雨の中で震えていた。
example.meaning: A pitiful puppy was trembling in the rain.

example.sentence: 可哀想な子供たちのために寄付をした。
example.meaning: I made a donation for the poor children.

example.sentence: 気の毒な境遇の人を見ると、胸が痛む。
example.meaning: My heart aches when I see someone in an unfortunate situation.
example.meaningKo: 딱한 처지의 사람을 보면 가슴이 아프다.

example.sentence: 鮮やかな色の花が咲いている。
example.meaning: Vivid-colored flowers are blooming.

example.sentence: 彼女は華やかなドレスを着ていた。
example.meaning: She was wearing a gorgeous dress.

example.sentence: 派手な服を着た人が目立っている。
example.meaning: A person wearing flashy clothes stands out.
example.meaningKo: 화려한 옷을 입은 사람이 눈에 띈다.

example.sentence: 彼は地味な服装を好む。
example.meaning: He prefers plain clothing.

example.sentence: 大人しい子供は先生に好かれる。
example.meaning: Quiet children are liked by teachers.

example.sentence: 彼はいつもでしゃばらないので、周りから信頼されている。
example.meaning: He is trusted by those around him because he never pushes himself forward.
example.meaningKo: 그는 항상 나서지 않기 때문에 주변에서 신뢰를 받고 있다.
furigana: はいつもでしゃばらないので、

example.sentence: 控えめな態度が彼女の魅力だ。
example.meaning: Her reserved attitude is her charm.

example.sentence: 彼はいつも会議でしゃしゃり出て、みんなを困らせる。
example.meaning: He always pushes himself forward in meetings and annoys everyone.
example.meaningKo: 그는 항상 회의에서 거만하게 나서서 모두를 곤란하게 한다.

example.sentence: 厚かましい人は何度断られても諦めない。
example.meaning: A shameless person never gives up no matter how many times they are refused.
example.meaningKo: 뻔뻔한 사람은 몇 번을 거절당해도 포기하지 않는다.

example.sentence: ずうずうしい人は自分の行動を恥ずかしいと思わない。
example.meaning: A brazen person does not feel ashamed of their own actions.
example.meaningKo: 뻔뻔스러운 사람은 자신의 행동을 부끄럽다고 생각하지 않는다.

example.sentence: けちな人はなかなか財布を開かない。
example.meaning: A stingy person rarely opens their wallet.
example.meaningKo: 인색한 사람은 좀처럼 지갑을 열지 않는다.

example.sentence: 臆病な彼は暗い道を一人で歩けない。
example.meaning: The timid him cannot walk alone on a dark road.
example.meaningKo: 소심한 그는 어두운 길을 혼자 걷지 못한다.

example.sentence: まじめくさった顔で冗談を言う。
example.meaning: He tells a joke with an overly serious face.

example.sentence: 堅物な上司は新しいアイデアをなかなか受け入れない。
example.meaning: My inflexible boss rarely accepts new ideas.
example.meaningKo: 고지식한 상사는 새로운 아이디어를 좀처럼 받아들이지 않는다.

example.sentence: 堅苦しい挨拶は抜きにして、気軽に話しましょう。
example.meaning: Let's skip the stiff formalities and talk casually.

example.sentence: くだけた雰囲気の店で友人と食事した。
example.meaning: I had a meal with a friend at a casual restaurant.
example.meaningKo: 격식 없는 분위기의 가게에서 친구와 식사했다.

example.sentence: 馴れ馴れしい態度は初対面の人に失礼だ。
example.meaning: An overly familiar attitude is rude to someone you meet for the first time.
example.meaningKo: 지나치게 스스럼없는 태도는 처음 만나는 사람에게 실례다.

example.sentence: 打ち解けた雰囲気の中でみんなが本音を話した。
example.meaning: Everyone spoke their true feelings in the friendly atmosphere.
example.meaningKo: 친밀해진 분위기 속에서 모두가 솔직한 속마음을 이야기했다.

example.sentence: 遠慮がない彼女はいつも思ったことをそのまま言う。
example.meaning: She, who holds nothing back, always says exactly what she thinks.
example.meaningKo: 거리낌 없는 그녀는 항상 생각한 것을 그대로 말한다.

example.sentence: 遠慮がちな彼は自分の意見をなかなか言えない。
example.meaning: The reserved him can hardly express his own opinion.
example.meaningKo: 조심스러운 그는 자신의 의견을 좀처럼 말하지 못한다.

72.meaning: in other words; that is to say
72.subject: 부사

example.sentence: それはとりも直さず失敗を意味する。
example.meaning: That is to say, it means failure.

example.sentence: 彼はすなわち私の兄だ。
example.meaning: He is, in other words, my older brother.

example.sentence: 彼は優秀だ。もっとも、努力も怠らない。
example.meaning: He is excellent. That said, he never neglects his efforts.
example.meaningKo: 그는 우수하다. 다만, 노력도 게을리하지 않는다.

example.sentence: 参加できる。ただし、事前に申し込みが必要だ。
example.meaning: You can participate. However, prior registration is required.
example.meaningKo: 참가할 수 있다. 단, 사전에 신청이 필요하다.

example.sentence: 彼女は歌のみならず、踊りも上手だ。
example.meaning: She is good not only at singing but also at dancing.

72.meaning: not only; of course; let alone
72.subject: 접속사

example.sentence: 彼は日本語はもとより、英語も話せる。
example.meaning: He can speak not only Japanese but also English.
example.meaningKo: 그는 일본어는 물론이고 영어도 말할 수 있다.

72.meaning: needless to say; it goes without saying
72.subject: 부사

example.sentence: 健康が言うまでもなく大切だ。
example.meaning: Needless to say, health is important.

72.meaning: let alone; far from; not to mention
72.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼は漢字はおろか、ひらがなも書けない。
example.meaning: He cannot even write hiragana, let alone kanji.
example.meaningKo: 그는 한자는커녕 히라가나도 쓸 수 없다.

72.meaning: would be acceptable; would be understandable if
72.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 一度はまだしも、何度も同じ失敗をするのは問題だ。
example.meaning: Once would be acceptable, but making the same mistake many times is a problem.
example.meaningKo: 한 번이라면 그나마 낫지만, 몇 번이나 같은 실수를 하는 것은 문제다.

example.sentence: 兄にひきかえ、弟はとても内気だ。
example.meaning: In contrast to his older brother, the younger brother is very shy.

example.sentence: 今日はかなり寒い。
example.meaning: It is quite cold today.

example.sentence: 彼はずいぶん背が高い。
example.meaning: He is considerably tall.

example.sentence: 彼女はよほど疲れていたのか、すぐ眠った。
example.meaning: She must have been quite tired, as she fell asleep right away.

example.sentence: 彼の成績は私よりはるかに優れている。
example.meaning: His grades are far superior to mine.

80.meaning: long ago; already; a long time since
80.subject: 時間副詞

example.sentence: その映画はとうに終わっていた。
example.meaning: That movie had already ended long ago.
example.meaningKo: 그 영화는 이미 오래전에 끝나 있었다.

example.sentence: 彼はとっくに家に帰った。
example.meaning: He already went home long ago.

example.sentence: 彼女はすでに部屋を出ていた。
example.meaning: She had already left the room.

example.sentence: その問題はもはや解決できない。
example.meaning: That problem can no longer be solved.
example.meaningKo: 그 문제는 이미 해결할 수 없다.

80.meaning: now; by now; at this point
80.subject: 時間副詞

example.sentence: 彼はいまや世界的に有名な選手だ。
example.meaning: He is now a world-famous athlete.

example.sentence: 今日ではスマートフォンが生活に欠かせない。
example.meaning: Nowadays, smartphones are indispensable in daily life.
example.meaningKo: 오늘날에는 스마트폰이 생활에 없어서는 안 된다.

81.meaning: all or nothing; sink or swim
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: いちかばちか、試験に挑戦してみた。
example.meaning: I took a chance and tried the exam.

81.meaning: sink or swim; now or never
81.meaningKo: 乗るか反るか/되든 안 되든
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: のるかそるか、この勝負に全力を尽くす。
example.meaning: It's now or never; I'll give everything to this match.
example.meaningKo: 되든 안 되든, 이 승부에 전력을 다한다.

81.kanji: 蜂の巣をつついたよう
81.meaning: like stirring up a hornet's nest; in an uproar
81.meaningKo: 蜂の巣をつついたよう/벌집을 건드린 듯
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: その知らせで、会場は蜂の巣をつついたような騒ぎになった。
example.meaning: The news caused an uproar in the venue, like stirring up a hornet's nest.
example.meaningKo: 그 소식으로 회장은 벌집을 건드린 듯한 소동이 되었다.

81.meaning: feeling bewildered; as if bewitched by a fox
81.meaningKo: 狐に摘まれたよう/여우에 홀린 것 같다
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 突然の出来事に、彼は狐につままれたような顔をしていた。
example.meaning: He had a bewildered look on his face at the sudden event.
example.meaningKo: 갑작스러운 일에 그는 여우에 홀린 듯한 표정을 짓고 있었다.

example.sentence: 高級な食器を彼に渡しても、猫に小判だ。
example.meaning: Giving him fine tableware is like casting pearls before swine.
example.meaningKo: 고급 식기를 그에게 줘도 돼지에 진주다.

81.meaning: to use a sprat to catch a mackerel; to get a great gain with little investment
81.meaningKo: 海老で鯛を釣る/새우로 도미를 낚다
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 小さなお土産で大きな契約を取るとは、まさに海老で鯛を釣るようなものだ。
example.meaning: Getting a big contract with a small gift is truly like using a sprat to catch a mackerel.
example.meaningKo: 작은 선물로 큰 계약을 따내다니, 말 그대로 새우로 도미를 낚는 격이다.

81.meaning: adding insult to injury; misfortunes never come alone
81.meaningKo: 泣き面に蜂/엎친 데 덮친 격
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 財布を失くした上に雨まで降ってきて、泣き面に蜂だ。
example.meaning: On top of losing my wallet, it started to rain — adding insult to injury.
example.meaningKo: 지갑을 잃어버린 데다 비까지 내리다니, 엎친 데 덮친 격이다.

81.meaning: a lucky break from a mistake; a silver lining
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 道に迷ったおかげで素敵なカフェを見つけた。怪我の功名だね。
example.meaning: Getting lost led me to find a wonderful café. That's a lucky break.
example.meaningKo: 길을 잃은 덕분에 멋진 카페를 발견했다. 전화위복이네.

81.meaning: something unexpected coming true; truth is stranger than fiction
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 冗談で言ったことが本当になるとは、瓢箪から駒だ。
example.meaning: What I said as a joke actually came true — truth is stranger than fiction.
example.meaningKo: 농담으로 한 말이 현실이 되다니, 호박에서 말이 나온 격이다.

81.meaning: even experts make mistakes; nobody is perfect
81.meaningKo: 猿も木から落ちる/원숭이도 나무에서 떨어진다
81.subject: 표현

example.sentence: あの料理人でも失敗することがある。猿も木から落ちるというものだ。
example.meaning: Even that chef makes mistakes sometimes. Nobody is perfect.
example.meaningKo: 저 요리사도 실패할 때가 있다. 원숭이도 나무에서 떨어진다는 말이 맞다.

82.meaning: a last-ditch desperate act; cornered animal's final struggle
82.meaningKo: イタチの最後っ屁/최후의 발악
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 負けそうになって暴言を吐くのは、いたちの最後っ屁だ。
example.meaning: Hurling insults when you're about to lose is just a last-ditch desperate act.
example.meaningKo: 질 것 같아지자 폭언을 퍼붓는 것은 최후의 발악이다.

82.meaning: the mouth is the source of disaster; loose lips sink ships
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 余計なことを言って誤解を招いてしまった。口は禍の元とはよく言ったものだ。
example.meaning: I said something unnecessary and caused a misunderstanding. It's true that loose lips sink ships.
example.meaningKo: 쓸데없는 말을 해서 오해를 샀다. 입이 화근이라는 말이 딱 맞다.

82.meaning: you won't know until you try; nothing ventured, nothing gained
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 物は試しで、新しい料理に挑戦してみた。
example.meaning: On the principle of nothing ventured nothing gained, I tried cooking a new dish.
example.meaningKo: 뭐든 해봐야 안다는 마음으로 새로운 요리에 도전해 봤다.

82.meaning: know your place; know your limits
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 実力もないのに横綱に挑むとは、身の程を知れというものだ。
example.meaning: Challenging a grand champion without any skill — he really needs to know his place.
example.meaningKo: 실력도 없으면서 요코즈나에게 도전하다니, 분수를 알아야 한다.

82.meaning: falling on deaf ears; like talking to a wall
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 彼に何度注意しても糠に釘で、全く改善されない。
example.meaning: No matter how many times I warn him, it falls on deaf ears and nothing improves.
example.meaningKo: 그에게 몇 번을 주의를 줘도 쇠귀에 경 읽기라 전혀 나아지지 않는다.

82.meaning: casting pearls before swine; like talking to a wall
82.meaningKo: 馬の耳に念仏/쇠귀에 경 읽기
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 息子に勉強しなさいと言っても、馬の耳に念仏だ。
example.meaning: Telling my son to study is like talking to a wall.
example.meaningKo: 아들에게 공부하라고 말해봤자 쇠귀에 경 읽기다.

82.meaning: perseverance pays off; if you sit on a stone for three years, it will become warm
82.meaningKo: 石の上にも三年/세 살 버릇 여든까지
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 石の上にも三年というから、もう少し辛抱してみよう。
example.meaning: They say perseverance pays off, so let's endure a little longer.
example.meaningKo: 참을 인 자 셋이면 살인도 피한다고 하니, 조금 더 참아 보자.

82.meaning: where there's a will, there's a way; you can do it if you try
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 為せばなるの精神で、難しい試験に合格することができた。
example.meaning: With the spirit of 'where there's a will, there's a way,' I was able to pass the difficult exam.
example.meaningKo: 하면 된다는 정신으로 어려운 시험에 합격할 수 있었다.

82.kanji: 塵も積もれば山となる
82.meaning: many a little makes a mickle; every little bit counts
82.meaningKo: 塵も積もれば山となる/티끌 모아 태산
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 塵も積もれば山となるというから、毎日少しずつ貯金している。
example.meaning: As they say, every little bit counts, so I save a little money every day.
example.meaningKo: 티끌 모아 태산이라고 하니, 매일 조금씩 저금하고 있다.

82.meaning: failure is the mother of success
82.meaningKo: 失敗は成功の基/실패는 성공의 어머니
82.subject: ことわざ

example.sentence: 失敗は成功の基というから、今回の失敗を糧に次は頑張ろう。
example.meaning: As they say, failure is the mother of success, so let's use this failure as a lesson and do better next time.
example.meaningKo: 실패는 성공의 어머니라고 하니, 이번 실패를 교훈 삼아 다음엔 열심히 하자.

example.sentence: 基本をしっかり身につけることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to firmly master the basics.

example.sentence: 応用が広い技術を学ぶ。
example.meaning: I learn a technology with wide applications.

example.sentence: 理論だけでなく実践も重要だ。
example.meaning: Not only theory, but practice is also important.

example.sentence: 実践を通じて知識が深まる。
example.meaning: Knowledge deepens through practice.

example.sentence: 研究方法を工夫することで、より良い結果が得られる。
example.meaning: By devising research methods, better results can be obtained.
example.meaningKo: 연구 방법을 고안함으로써 더 좋은 결과를 얻을 수 있다.

91.meaning: building an argument, establishing a thesis
91.subject: 명사

example.sentence: 立論が明確でなければ、議論は進まない。
example.meaning: If the argument is not clear, the discussion will not proceed.
example.meaningKo: 입론이 명확하지 않으면 논의가 진행되지 않는다.

example.sentence: 反論に対して冷静に答える必要がある。
example.meaning: It is necessary to respond calmly to counterarguments.
example.meaningKo: 반론에 대해 냉정하게 답할 필요가 있다.

example.sentence: 主張を裏付ける証拠を示すことが重要だ。
example.meaning: It is important to present evidence to support one's claim.
example.meaningKo: 주장을 뒷받침하는 증거를 제시하는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 根拠のない主張は説得力がない。
example.meaning: A claim without grounds has no persuasive power.

example.sentence: 証拠が集まり、事件の真相が明らかになった。
example.meaning: Evidence was gathered and the truth of the incident became clear.

example.sentence: 彼女はデータの分析を行った。
example.meaning: She conducted an analysis of the data.

example.sentence: この表は二つのグループの対比を示している。
example.meaning: This table shows the contrast between two groups.
